code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of building‑related triples into a single fluent sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase; placeholders {subj} and {obj} will be replaced.
    # Templates that include the subject (used when subjects differ)
    templates_with_subj = {
        "architecturalStyle": "{subj} has an architectural style of {obj}",
        "buildingStartDate": "{subj} started construction in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "{subj} was completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "{subj} has {obj} floors",
        "location": "{subj} is located at {obj}",
        "cost": "{subj} cost {obj}",
        "floorArea": "{subj} has a floor area of {obj}",
        "architect": "{subj} was designed by {obj}",
        "owner": "{subj} is owned by {obj}",
        "formerName": "{subj} was formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "{subj} is {obj} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "{subj} is a {obj}",
        "developer": "{subj} was developed by {obj}",
        "tenant": "{subj} has tenant {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "{subj} is part of {obj}",
        "country": "{subj} is in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "{subj} currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "{subj} has address {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "{subj} was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "{subj} was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "{subj} died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "{subj} has {obj} beds",
        "region": "{subj} is in the region {obj}",
        "state": "{subj} is in the state {obj}",
        "website": "{subj}'s website is {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "{subj} was constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "{subj} has NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "{subj} was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "{subj} is known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "{subj} is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "The leader of {subj} is {obj}",
        "chancellor": "The chancellor of {subj} is {obj}",
        "governmentType": "{subj} has a government type of {obj}",
        "capital": "The capital of {subj} is {obj}",
        "language": "{subj} uses the {obj} language",
        "leaderTitle": "{subj} has the leader title {obj}",
        "currency": "{subj} uses the {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{subj} has an ethnic group of {obj}",
        "origin": "{subj} originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "{subj} has a significant project called {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "{subj} was founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "People from {subj} are called {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "{subj} has {obj} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "{subj} has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "{subj} has architecture {obj}",
        "postalCode": "{subj} has postal code {obj}",
    }

    # Templates without the subject (used when all triples share the same subject)
    templates_no_subj = {
        "architecturalStyle": "has an architectural style of {obj}",
        "buildingStartDate": "started construction in {obj}",
        "completionDate": "was completed in {obj}",
        "floorCount": "has {obj} floors",
        "location": "is located at {obj}",
        "cost": "costs {obj}",
        "floorArea": "has a floor area of {obj}",
        "architect": "was designed by {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "formerName": "was formerly known as {obj}",
        "height": "is {obj} metres tall",
        "buildingType": "is a {obj}",
        "developer": "was developed by {obj}",
        "tenant": "has tenant {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "currentTenants": "currently houses {obj}",
        "address": "has address {obj}",
        "inaugurationDate": "was inaugurated on {obj}",
        "birthPlace": "was born in {obj}",
        "deathPlace": "died in {obj}",
        "bedCount": "has {obj} beds",
        "region": "is in the region {obj}",
        "state": "is in the state {obj}",
        "website": "'s website is {obj}",
        "yearOfConstruction": "was constructed in {obj}",
        "NationalRegisterOfHistoricPlacesReferenceNumber": "has NRHP reference number {obj}",
        "addedToTheNationalRegisterOfHistoricPlaces": "was added to the NRHP on {obj}",
        "significantBuilding": "is known for the building {obj}",
        "governingBody": "is governed by {obj}",
        "leader": "the leader is {obj}",
        "chancellor": "the chancellor is {obj}",
        "governmentType": "has a government type of {obj}",
        "capital": "the capital is {obj}",
        "language": "uses the {obj} language",
        "leaderTitle": "has the leader title {obj}",
        "currency": "uses the {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "has an ethnic group of {obj}",
        "origin": "originates from {obj}",
        "significantProject": "has a significant project called {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "was founded in {obj}",
        "demonym": "people are called {obj}",
        "numberOfRooms": "has {obj} rooms",
        "keyPerson": "has key person {obj}",
        "architecture": "has architecture {obj}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
    }

    # Determine whether all triples share the same subject
    subjects = {t.subject for t in triples}
    use_common_subject = len(subjects) == 1
    common_subject = triples[0].subject if use_common_subject else None

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        if use_common_subject:
            tmpl = templates_no_subj.get(triple.predicate)
        else:
            tmpl = templates_with_subj.get(triple.predicate)

        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subj=triple.subject, obj=triple.object)
        else:
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Build the final sentence
    if not clauses:
        return ""

    if use_common_subject:
        # Prepend the common subject once
        sentence_body = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + (", and " if len(clauses) > 1 else "") + clauses[-1]
        sentence = f"{common_subject} {sentence_body}."
    else:
        if len(clauses) == 1:
            sentence = clauses[0] + "."
        else:
            sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    clauses = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subj=triple.subject, obj=triple.object)
        else:
            # Fallback generic rendering
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {triple.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Join clauses into a single fluent sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    # Capitalize first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
